content.text: Armor is Temp HP with built-in damage reduction. Strong while it lasts. Catastrophic when it breaks.
Damage Flow
When a hit lands, damage passes through four layers:

1. Per-Die Reduction: Subtract armor Value from each damage die individually. Min 0 per die. 2. Absorb Threshold: Sum remaining damage. Subtract Absorb from total. Flat threshold. 3. Punch-Through: Remaining damage hits BOTH Armor HP AND Character HP. 4. Armor Break: When Armor HP reaches 0, all layers (Value, Absorb, HP) drop to zero. Wearer fully exposed. Permanent max HP loss to armor.

content.label: Bounce Rule
content.text: If a hit deals 0 after per-die reduction, it never reaches Absorb and never degrades armor. Light weapons bouncing off heavy plate don't wear it down.
Armor Table

Note: Light Weave and Padded Jacket have Armor HP but no Absorb. Against multi-die weapons, damage exceeding per-die Value hits both armor HP and flesh simultaneously. Environmental effects, acid, and abilities that target armor directly also interact with Armor HP at all tiers.
Rift-Walker Suit — Same protection as Contractor Standard but with built-in toxicity dispersal: -1 toxicity per rift encounter.
Siege Rig — Exoskeletal powered armor. Walking fortress. Requires Chrome 6 to operate.
Armor Break = Liability
When armor breaks, the tank's stat investment works against them. Chrome and Grit don't help dodge. Low evasion makes them the easiest target on the field with no defensive layers.
Economic Loop

• Evasion builds: No repair costs. High medical costs when hits land. Scars accumulate. • Tank builds: High repair costs every contract. Low medical while armor holds. Catastrophic if armor breaks mid-contract.
Both paths drain the wallet. Money is survival. Every choice feeds the debt cycle.
Armor Repair & Durability
Field Repair (Armor HP > 0)

• Requirement: 1 repair charge (Crafting Kit or Field Workshop) • Check: Crafting T1, DC 6 • Success: Restore 1d4+1 Armor HP (cannot exceed current max) • Time: 1 AP out of combat, or 10 minutes narrative • Can attempt multiple times. Each attempt costs 1 charge.
Emergency Repair (Armor HP = 0, Broken)

• Requirement: 3 repair charges • Check: Crafting T1, DC 8 • Success: Armor restored to 50% of CURRENT max HP. Value and Absorb return to full. • PERMANENT PENALTY: Max Armor HP reduced by 25% (round up, minimum 1). Structural integrity compromised. • Failure: Charges consumed. Armor remains broken. Can retry with more charges. • After emergency repair, standard field repair works normally to top up from 50%.
Reforge (Workshop, Downtime)
• Requirement: Crafting T2+ and a Workshop • Cost: 50% of armor purchase price in materials • Effect: Restores ALL max HP to original value. Removes all break penalties. 1 downtime activity.
Break Penalty Accumulation
Heavy Plate example:

rows: Compromised. Reforge 600 cr or fight degraded.

content.text: Nano-Repair Lattice (Armor Augment)

• Cost: 800 cr. Takes 1 armor mod slot. • Effect: When armor breaks (hits 0 HP), NO permanent max HP loss. • Still requires: Emergency repair (3 charges, DC 8) to restore function. • Break-even: Saves 600 cr reforge on Heavy Plate after first break. Pays for itself immediately on expensive armor.
Repair Economics Per Contract

content.label: Siege Rig Insurance
content.text: Siege Rig break costs 1,700 cr to fully restore (~70% of purchase price). Nano-Repair Lattice (800 cr) eliminates the reforge, reducing break cost to just 450 cr in charges.
Shields
Shields provide an Evasion bonus instead of Armor reduction. They occupy one hand.

rows: Can use Light weapon in shield hand
-1 accuracy on all attacks. Provides cover to allies behind you.
